projectDescription: A system daemon that enables automatic, safe, and reliable firmware updates on Linux via the Linux Vendor Firmware Service (LVFS).
keyFeatures: Automatic firmware updates via the Linux Vendor Firmware Service (LVFS)
Support for 100+ device types including USB, NVMe, Thunderbolt, UEFI capsule, and more
Plugin architecture enabling vendor-specific update protocols
Host Security ID (HSI) for firmware security posture evaluation
BIOS settings management via kernel firmware-attributes API
Device emulation for regression testing without physical hardware
D-Bus interface for GUI frontends (GNOME Software, KDE Discover, etc.)
Enterprise firmware approval and best-known-configuration (BKC) support
Cross-platform support including Windows (subset of plugins)
Peer-to-peer metadata sharing via Passim to reduce bandwidth
publicApiSurface: FwupdClient — D-Bus client for interacting with the fwupd daemon
FwupdDevice — Represents a device managed by fwupd
FwupdRelease — Represents a firmware release available for a device
FwupdRemote — Represents a metadata source (e.g. LVFS)
FwupdSecurityAttr — Represents a host security attribute (HSI)
FwupdBiosSetting — Represents a BIOS firmware setting
FuPlugin — Base class for all fwupd plugins
FuDevice — Base class for all devices managed by plugins
FuFirmware — Base class for firmware file parsers
FuUdevDevice / FuUsbDevice / FuBluezDevice — Device base types
fwupdmgr — CLI client for end users
fwupdtool — Standalone CLI for plugin developers and debugging
